subject: Chemistry
title: Fluorine: The Most Reactive Element
description: A fast breakdown of Fluorine: The Most Reactive Element for builders.

hook.text: Fluorine reacts with almost everything—including glass, water, and materials already 'fully' bonded.

buildup.text: It's the most electronegative element. It rips electrons from nearly any atom it touches.

discovery.text: Isolating fluorine took 75 years. Multiple chemists were poisoned, blinded, or killed trying.

twist.text: Henri Moissan finally isolated it in 1886 using electrolysis. He won the Nobel Prize—and lost his health.

climax.text: Fluorine compounds include Teflon, refrigerants, and toothpaste. Tamed, it's incredibly useful.

punchline.text: Reacts with almost anything. Deadly to isolate, essential when tamed.

quiz.question: Why was fluorine so dangerous to isolate?
options: It reacts violently with almost everything, including the equipment used
It is radioactive and emits lethal radiation
